description: Tempdoc 575 §15 + §17 Face C — the in-flight liveness projection register (Pillar 3b's anti-drift teeth), GENERALIZED across liveness domains. Every in-flight record's displayed RUNNING/'stalled' state must derive from its domain's ONE liveness authority, never from raw stream membership or an inline timestamp comparison. Each `domains` entry names an authority (module + exported symbol) + its registered render sites. The check enforces (1) positive coverage — every registered render site imports its domain's authority symbol; (2) a negative early-warning (indexing-job domain only) — an unregistered site mapping a job 'PROCESSING' state to a 'running' presentation without the authority is flagged. HONEST SCOPE (565 §12.10): import-visible early-warning over the ui-web shell tree — a re-model that computes the state name or maps a different literal is import-invisible and slips; register + discipline, the same ceiling as check-run-renderers / check-controls-a11y. The cross-domain generalization (each liveness thing — indexing job (heartbeat-lease) / brain install (polled-state) / backend connection (multi-source positive-contact, tempdoc 649) — derives from a DECLARED liveness owner) is 575 §17 Face C, replacing the old single-domain scope note.

indexing-job._doc: Heartbeat-lease model: a PROCESSING job is RUNNING only while its heartbeat is fresh.

brain-install._doc: Polled-state model: a running install/pack with a stale backend timestamp shows 'stalled'.

connection._doc: Tempdoc 649 — multi-source positive-contact model: the backend is REACHABLE iff a positive contact (a poll success OR any SSE frame/heartbeat) occurred within the watchdog window. The sibling of the heartbeat-lease (indexing-job) and polled-state (brain-install) models — reachability must derive from this ONE authority, never from an inline poll-timestamp comparison (the bug 649 fixes). aiStateStore is the sole render site (feeds the verdict); phantom-check N/A (its PROCESSING/running regex is indexing-job-specific).
